channel.title: Iro AI Blog
channel.description: Short, practical writing on AI fluency, prompting, AI tools, and the active-practice method that beats endless video courses.

item.title: How to actually learn ChatGPT in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Most people learn ChatGPT by random experimentation and hit a ceiling. Here's the active-practice method that builds judgment instead of just memorising prompts.

item.title: The 7 prompt patterns that work everywhere
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Forget magic phrases. These 7 prompt patterns transfer across models and survive model upgrades — the foundation of practical prompt engineering.

item.title: How to spot AI hallucinations in 5 seconds
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI models confidently invent facts, citations, and numbers. Here are the five-second checks that catch most hallucinations — and a longer set for when stakes are high.

item.title: AI agents, explained without the jargon
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Strip away the hype: an AI agent is a system that pursues a goal through multiple steps, often using tools. Here's what they really do, when they're worth using, and where they predictably break.

item.title: The best free AI tools for daily work in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: A 2026-up-to-date guide to free-tier AI tools that are actually worth using for daily work — what each is good for, where they fall short, and how to combine them.

item.title: AI for beginners: a 30-day learning plan
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: A beginner-friendly 30-day plan to learn AI from scratch: what to practise each week, which free tools to use, and how to build a daily habit that actually sticks.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini in 2026: what each is genuinely best at, where each falls short, and how to pick the right one for writing, research, coding, and everyday work.

item.title: Why your AI prompts aren't working (and how to fix them)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: If ChatGPT keeps giving you generic, useless answers, the prompt is usually the problem. Here are the five most common prompt mistakes and how to fix each one fast.

item.title: AI automation for beginners: 5 things to automate this week
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: A beginner's guide to AI automation: five high-value, no-code tasks you can automate this week, plus how to tell when a simple workflow beats a fancy AI agent.

item.title: Iro AI vs Coursiv: which AI learning app is right for you in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: An honest Iro AI vs Coursiv comparison for 2026: how the two AI learning apps differ on teaching style, pricing, free trials, and who each one is actually best for.

item.title: Iro AI vs Learnova: which AI learning app should you pick in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: An honest Iro AI vs Learnova comparison for 2026: how the two AI learning apps differ on teaching style, hands-on practice, pricing, and who each one suits best.

item.title: Iro AI vs Brilliant: which should you use to actually get good at AI?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: An honest Iro AI vs Brilliant comparison for 2026: Brilliant teaches the math and concepts behind AI; Iro AI teaches you to use AI tools for real work. Which fits your goal.

item.title: Iro AI vs Duolingo: the "Duolingo for AI," explained
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Iro AI vs Duolingo in 2026: how the gamified-learning method that made Duolingo famous applies to learning AI tools — and why they're not actually competitors.

item.title: What is AI fluency? A practical definition (and how to build it)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI fluency is the practical ability to use, direct, and evaluate AI tools to get better results. Here is a clear definition, a 4-layer framework, and how to build it in 5 minutes a day.

item.title: What is prompt engineering? A clear definition with examples
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Prompt engineering is the skill of structuring AI instructions to get reliably better output. A plain-English definition, why it works, before-and-after examples, and whether it still matters in 2026.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Claude: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Claude in 2026: an honest, side-by-side comparison of strengths, pricing, and who each is best for — so you can pick the right AI assistant (or use both well).

item.title: How to use AI for studying (without cheating yourself)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use AI for studying the right way: the 5 best ways to study with ChatGPT or Claude, what to avoid so you actually learn, and copy-paste study prompts.

item.title: How to learn AI without coding (a no-code path for 2026)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: You don't need to code to learn AI. Here's the no-code path to AI fluency in 2026 — the tools, the skills, and a five-minute-a-day routine that works for non-technical people.

item.title: What is an LLM? Large language models explained simply
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is an LLM? A large language model is an AI trained on text to predict the next word. Here's how LLMs work, examples, what they're bad at, and how to use them well — in plain English.

item.title: How long does it take to learn AI? A realistic timeline
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How long does it take to learn AI? With five minutes of daily practice, you can be useful in two to three weeks. Here's a realistic week-by-week timeline and how to learn AI faster.

item.title: The 7 AI skills worth learning in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The 7 AI skills worth learning in 2026 — prompting, tool choice, judgment, verification, workflow, agents, and continuous learning — why they're durable, and how to build each one.

item.title: 15 ChatGPT prompts for beginners (copy, paste, and learn)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: 15 ChatGPT prompts for beginners to copy and paste — for work, learning, and everyday life — plus the simple structure that makes any prompt better. No experience needed.

item.title: AI agents vs. automation: what's the difference (and when to use each)?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI agents vs automation explained: automation follows fixed rules, an AI agent decides its own steps toward a goal. Learn the difference, see examples, and know when to use each.

item.title: How to use AI at work (a practical guide for 2026)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use AI at work in 2026: the best starting tasks, how to do it responsibly (verify, protect data, follow policy), and how to build the habit. A practical guide for any role.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Gemini: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Gemini in 2026: an honest comparison of strengths, free tiers, ecosystems, and the best pick for each task. Plus the one skill that matters more than the tool you choose.

item.title: The best AI apps in 2026 (and what each is best for)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The best AI apps in 2026, compared by what each is best for — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, Copilot, and Iro. A practical shortlist for everyday users, not a hype list.

item.title: What is generative AI? A simple explanation with examples
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is generative AI? A plain-English explanation: how it works, real examples (text, images, video, code), how it differs from traditional AI, its limits, and how to use it well.

item.title: What is agentic AI? Agents explained simply (2026)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is agentic AI? A plain-English explanation of AI agents in 2026 — how they differ from chatbots and generative AI, real examples, when they're useful, and the risks to manage.

item.title: How to write a good AI prompt: a step-by-step guide
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to write a good AI prompt, step by step: the context-role-task-format recipe, before-and-after examples, and how to refine answers. Works on ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini.

item.title: The AI Fluency Pyramid: the 5 levels of getting good at AI
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The AI Fluency Pyramid is a 5-level framework for getting good at AI — Access, Operation, Direction, Judgment, and Leverage. Find your level and learn how to climb to the next.

item.title: Claude vs Gemini: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Claude vs Gemini in 2026: an honest comparison of writing, coding, reasoning, free tiers, and Google integration — plus the best pick for each task and what matters more than the tool.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Perplexity: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Perplexity in 2026: assistant vs answer engine. Compare strengths, citations, free tiers, and the best pick for research vs creating — plus why many people use both.

item.title: Will AI take your job? An honest answer (and how to stay ahead)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Will AI take your job? An honest answer: AI changes most jobs more than it eliminates them, and the real risk is being out-competed by people who use AI well. Here's how to future-proof your career.

item.title: What is AI literacy? A clear definition for 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is AI literacy? A clear 2026 definition: understanding what AI is, how it works, its limits, and how to use it responsibly — how it differs from AI fluency, and how to build it.

item.title: How to use ChatGPT: a beginner's guide for 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use ChatGPT in 2026: a step-by-step beginner's guide — how to start, what to use it for, how to write prompts that work, and the mistakes to avoid.

item.title: AI for small business: practical ways to use it in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI for small business in 2026: practical, no-code ways to use AI for marketing, customer service, admin, and analysis — the best starting tasks, free tools, and how to do it safely.

item.title: How to tell if something is AI-generated (text, images & more)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to tell if something is AI-generated: the real tells in AI text and images, why detection tools are unreliable, and the verification habit that actually works.

item.title: AI vs machine learning vs deep learning: the difference, explained
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI vs machine learning vs deep learning explained simply: clear definitions, how they're related (nested, not separate), real examples, and where generative AI and ChatGPT fit.

item.title: The best AI tools for students in 2026 (and how to use them honestly)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The best AI tools for students in 2026 — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity and more — compared by use, plus how to use AI to study honestly without cheating.

item.title: The best ChatGPT alternatives in 2026 (and when to use each)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The best ChatGPT alternatives in 2026 — Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, and Copilot — compared by what each does better than ChatGPT, with a side-by-side table and how to choose.

item.title: How to use Claude: a beginner's guide for 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use Claude in 2026: a beginner's guide to Anthropic's AI — how to start, what it's best at, how to prompt it well, and how it compares to ChatGPT.

item.title: How to use Gemini: a beginner's guide for 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use Gemini in 2026: a beginner's guide to Google's AI — how to start, using it inside Google apps, prompting tips, and how it compares to ChatGPT.

item.title: What is RAG (retrieval-augmented generation)? Explained simply
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is RAG (retrieval-augmented generation)? A plain-English explanation: how it works, why it reduces hallucinations and adds citations, real examples, and why it matters.

item.title: AI for teachers: practical, responsible ways to use it in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI for teachers in 2026: practical, responsible ways to use AI for lesson planning, feedback, differentiation, and quizzes — plus how to handle student data and academic integrity.

item.title: ChatGPT vs Microsoft Copilot: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs Microsoft Copilot in 2026: an honest comparison of versatility vs Microsoft 365 integration, with a side-by-side table and the best pick for your workflow.

item.title: The best Coursiv alternatives in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Looking for a Coursiv alternative? Compare the best options for learning AI in 2026 — Iro AI, Brilliant, DataCamp, Khan Academy and more — by how you learn and how you pay.

item.title: The best Learnova alternatives in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Looking for a Learnova alternative? Compare the best apps for learning AI in 2026 — Iro AI, Brilliant, Khan Academy, DataCamp — by how you learn and how you pay.

item.title: How to use Perplexity: a beginner's guide for 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use Perplexity in 2026: a beginner's guide to the AI answer engine — how to start, why it cites sources, what it's best for, and how it compares to ChatGPT.

item.title: Gemini vs Perplexity: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Gemini vs Perplexity in 2026: assistant vs answer engine. Compare strengths, citations, Google integration, and the best pick for research vs creating — plus why many use both.

item.title: The best AI image generators in 2026 (and how to choose)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The best AI image generators in 2026 — Midjourney, ChatGPT/DALL·E, Adobe Firefly, Google Imagen, Stable Diffusion and more — compared by what each is best for, plus how to prompt them.

item.title: AI trends in 2026: what's actually changing (and what to do about it)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI trends in 2026: agentic AI, multimodal models, AI in everyday apps, and AI fluency as a baseline skill. What's really changing — and the practical way to stay ahead.

item.title: What is DeepSeek? The low-cost AI model, explained
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is DeepSeek? A plain-English explainer on the low-cost AI model — what it is, what it's good at, how it compares to ChatGPT, and what to consider before using it.

item.title: ChatGPT vs DeepSeek: which should you use in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: ChatGPT vs DeepSeek in 2026: an honest comparison of capability, cost, ecosystem, openness, and data considerations — with a side-by-side table and how to choose.

item.title: What is Grok? xAI's chatbot, explained
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is Grok? A plain-English explainer on xAI's chatbot — what it is, its real-time X integration, its personality, what it's good at, and how it compares to ChatGPT.

item.title: AI for real estate: practical ways agents can use it in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: AI for real estate in 2026: practical ways agents can use AI for listings, lead follow-up, market summaries, and admin — plus fair-housing compliance and data cautions.

item.title: How to use AI to write a resume (without sounding like a robot)
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use AI to write a resume in 2026: a step-by-step guide to tailoring it to a job, strengthening bullet points, and passing keyword filters — honestly, without fabricating anything.

item.title: We tested AI literacy on 50 people. The average score was 5/10.
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What's the average score on an AI literacy test? Across our first 50 AI IQ tests the average was 5.2/10 and 56% scored 5 or below. See the full score distribution and what it means.

item.title: Is prompt engineering dead in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: Is prompt engineering dead in 2026? Short answer: no, it evolved. Here's what changed, what still matters, and why clear prompting is now a core part of AI fluency.

item.title: How to use Grok: a beginner’s guide
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use Grok, xAI's chatbot: where to access it, its real-time strengths, prompting tips, and when to reach for it versus ChatGPT or Claude.

item.title: How to use DeepSeek: a beginner’s guide
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: How to use DeepSeek: where to access it, what it's good at, prompting tips, privacy considerations, and how it compares to ChatGPT.

item.title: The best AI coding tools in 2026
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: The best AI coding tools in 2026 compared — GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Claude Code, ChatGPT, and Gemini — what each is best at and how to use them well.

item.title: What is a context window in AI?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What is a context window in AI? A plain-English explainer: what it is, how it relates to tokens, why models 'forget', and practical tips for working within it.

item.title: What are tokens in AI?
item.dc:creator: Iro AI
item.description: What are tokens in AI? A plain-English explainer: what a token is, how tokenization works, the words-per-token rule of thumb, and why tokens drive cost and limits.
